data.title: Tsit Wing Wins Strong Shareholder Backing at 2026 AGM
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Tsit Wing International Holdings Limited ( (HK:2119) ).

Tsit Wing International Holdings Limited reported that all resolutions proposed at its annual general meeting held on 28 April 2026 were duly passed by shareholders by way of poll, confirming strong support for the board and existing governance structure. Shareholders approved the 2025 audited financial statements, a final dividend of HK1.36 cents per share, the re-election of four directors, the authorization for the board to fix directors’ remuneration, and the re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor, reinforcing continuity in management, oversight and capital return policy.

All directors of the company attended the meeting in person, and there were 720,731,512 issued shares entitled to vote, with no shareholders required to abstain or indicating opposition to the resolutions. The near-unanimous voting results, verified by Tricor Investor Services Limited as scrutineer, signal broad shareholder confidence in Tsit Wing’s leadership, financial reporting and dividend strategy, which may support the company’s stability and market perception among investors.

More about Tsit Wing International Holdings Limited

Tsit Wing International Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda, with its shares traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 2119. The group operates through a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and maintains its shareholder base via annual general meetings and formal voting procedures.

Average Trading Volume: 237,318

Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2289) ) has provided an announcement.

Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. has announced an updated final dividend for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, declaring a payout of RMB 0.3 per share, with the exact Hong Kong dollar amount and exchange rate to be confirmed. The dividend is subject to shareholder approval on 22 May 2026, with an ex-dividend date of 27 May, a record date of 5 June, and payment scheduled for 25 June 2026.

The company has detailed the withholding tax arrangements for different categories of shareholders, setting a 10% tax rate for non-resident enterprise and individual shareholders and a 20% rate for PRC-resident individual shareholders. These tax rules, aligned with PRC laws and relevant tax treaties, clarify net dividend entitlements and may influence the after-tax returns and planning considerations of both domestic and overseas investors in the company’s H shares.

More about Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Class H

Charmacy Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed pharmaceutical company operating in the healthcare sector. It is engaged in the distribution and sale of pharmaceutical products, serving both institutional and individual customers, with its H shares traded on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 02289.

data.title: GDH Guangnan Swings to Quarterly Loss on Weaker Foodstuffs and Tinplating Margins
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

GDH Guangnan (Holdings) Limited ( (HK:1203) ) has shared an update.

GDH Guangnan (Holdings) Limited reported unaudited first‑quarter 2026 revenue of HK$2.53 billion, down 12% year on year, swinging to a loss attributable to shareholders of HK$8.61 million as operating profit more than halved and total assets fell 6%, though shareholder equity edged up 2%. The fresh and live foodstuffs segment remained the main revenue driver but saw profit sharply reduced by lower swine prices, scaled‑back low‑efficiency trading and losses at an associate pig farming business, while the tinplating division faced intense price and volume pressure, underscoring a tougher operating environment that may weigh on margins and returns for investors in the near term.

More about GDH Guangnan (Holdings) Limited

GDH Guangnan (Holdings) Limited is a Hong Kong‑incorporated company with core operations in fresh and live foodstuffs and tinplating. Its food division focuses on live pig supply and slaughtering in Mainland China’s Greater Bay Area and Hong Kong, while the tinplating business produces and sells tinplate products into a highly competitive industrial market.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

i-CABLE Communications has called its annual general meeting for 23 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will receive the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and consider a slate of routine corporate governance matters. The agenda includes the re-election of several executive and independent non-executive directors, approval for the board to set directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and authorisations for the board to buy back up to 10% of the company’s shares and to issue additional shares, potentially enhancing capital management flexibility and influencing future shareholder returns.

The proposed share buy-back mandate would allow i-CABLE’s board to repurchase a limited portion of its issued share capital on the open market during the authorised period, while the general mandate to issue new shares or dispose of treasury shares would equip the company with tools to raise capital or optimise its balance sheet if needed. These resolutions, if approved, would align i-CABLE with common Hong Kong corporate practices, reinforcing board discretion over capital structure and signalling continued reliance on established external auditing and governance frameworks that may reassure investors about oversight and transparency.

More about i-CABLE Communications

i-CABLE Communications Limited is a Hong Kong-based communications and media company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. It operates in the telecommunications and broadcasting industry, providing cable TV, broadband and related services to consumers and businesses in Hong Kong, with a focus on subscription-based media and connectivity offerings in the local market.

An announcement from FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited ( (HK:2516) ) is now available.

FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited has completed the first stage of a major acquisition, becoming the sole shareholder of COPE Holding and Hyperlining Holding after all conditions under the transaction agreement were satisfied. Through these holdings and a direct stake held by FAR Luxembourg Holdings Sarl, the group now controls 51% of COPE and 51% of Hyperlining, turning the target companies into subsidiaries whose financial results will be consolidated into the group’s accounts.

The move marks a significant expansion of FAR International’s consolidated asset base and operational footprint, as majority ownership of COPE and Hyperlining is expected to strengthen its control over these businesses and their contributions to group performance. The company signalled that further announcements will follow on its planned shareholding increase and final equity acquisition steps, indicating a phased strategy to deepen its ownership and potentially reshape its capital structure and earnings profile.

More about FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited

FAR International Holdings Group Company Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2516. The group operates through multiple subsidiaries and uses holding structures, including FAR Luxembourg Holdings Sarl, to manage and consolidate its investments and equity interests in target businesses.

Bank of Qingdao Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3866) ) has issued an update.

Bank of Qingdao reported solid growth for the first quarter of 2026, with operating income rising 7.43% year-on-year to RMB 4.34 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders increasing 21.16% to RMB 1.52 billion. Basic and diluted earnings per share climbed to RMB 0.26, while annualized weighted average return on net assets improved to 14.63%, reflecting enhanced profitability.

The bank’s balance sheet also strengthened, as total assets expanded 2.36% from the end of 2025 to RMB 834.20 billion and equity attributable to shareholders grew 3.61% to RMB 50.94 billion, lifting net assets per share to RMB 7.31. Net cash flows from operating activities swung sharply into positive territory at RMB 12.78 billion, signaling improved liquidity and operational cash generation that should support ongoing growth and resilience for stakeholders.

The latest announcement is out from Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. Class H ( (HK:2429) ).

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. has renewed and expanded its cooperation with Alipay.com by signing a new Payment Services Framework Agreement and an Advertising Cooperation Framework Agreement, effective from January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2028. Under these arrangements, Alipay will continue to provide payment services enabling online transactions on UBOX’s vending machines, while both parties will collaborate on advertising and promoting Alipay’s payment products on the machines.

Because Alipay.com is an associate of a substantial shareholder of UBOX through Ant Group, the deals are classified as continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong listing rules. The size of the annual caps triggers reporting, announcement and annual review requirements, but the transactions are exempt from independent shareholders’ approval, streamlining compliance while formalizing a key strategic relationship in UBOX’s payments and advertising ecosystem.

More about Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. Class H

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. operates smart vending machines and related online transaction infrastructure in China, providing automated retail services that rely heavily on digital payment channels. Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2409) ) has issued an update.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. has called its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Qingdao, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the reports of the board and auditor. They will also vote on board changes, including the re-election of an independent non-executive director, the appointment of two new executive directors, and authorization for the board to set directors’ pay, alongside the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor.

Shareholders will further consider granting the board general mandates to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares, including the ability to hold repurchased shares as treasury stock, and to issue additional shares or dispose of treasury shares within defined limits. These mandates, if approved, would give the board additional flexibility in capital management and equity issuance during the mandate period, potentially influencing the company’s capital structure and shareholder value.
